lifecycle_state.description: Durable-memory lifecycle state on the post-promotion segment of the canonical promotion axis (observed/candidate/promoted/deprecated/archived; see promotion-event.v1 and DEC-002). A durable record starts 'promoted'; 'deprecated' = kept for history but no longer authoritative; 'archived' = retired. Distinct from any domain status field. Optional and additive.

promoted_at.description: When this record was promoted to durable memory; corresponds to a promotion-event.v1 into 'promoted'.

deprecated_at.description: When this record was deprecated (no longer authoritative). Present once lifecycle_state has reached 'deprecated'.

archived_at.description: When this record was archived/retired. Present when lifecycle_state is 'archived'.

superseded_by.description: Stable id of the record that replaces this one. Supersession is expressed by this pointer, not a lifecycle_state value; a superseded record is typically also 'deprecated'.

source_evidence_refs.description: References to the source evidence this durable record was promoted from (e.g. memory-observation.v1 ids, inbox item ids, vault-relative notes). Satisfies the SSOT exit criterion that every promoted durable item traces back to source evidence. Never absolute/machine-specific.
